events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess drywall finishing: joints not staggered. In commercial construction, drywall panels should be staggered so that butt joints and end joints don't align continuously across framing; reduces cracking, weak points, taping issues. GA-216 (Application and Finishing of Gypsum Panel Products) and IRC likely require staggered end joints not less than one framing cavity? Need decide fail because non-compliant. Work stage rough-complete. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 60? Value maybe 0 because no numeric reading asked. Confidence 0.9. Findings: end/butt joints aligned in continuous line rather than staggered; violates GA-216/IRC. Actions: remove/replace panels to stagger joints; verify layout before taping. Rationale. References: GA-216, IRC R702.3.5? Actually IRC maybe R702.3.5 Gypsum board installation: joints staggered. Need include. Ensure JSON valid.

  "rationale": "GA-216 and IRC require gypsum board end joints to be staggered so they do not align continuously across framing. The observed condition of un-staggered joints creates a non-compliant installation with increased risk of cracking and finish failure, so the work fails at this rough-complete stage.",
